src-2010: Install the latest Bloom under a standard (non-admin) user account and confirm it installs without admin privileges.
src-2005: Set FEEDBACK=FALSE and run a clean new-machine (VM) install of Bloom.
src-2020: Install the latest Bloom on a clean machine or VM and run a smoke test to confirm a clean install.
src-2021: Placeholder case (TC2) with no explicit test steps beyond setting the OS locale.
src-2030: Install the latest Bloom on an admin account with User Account Control enabled and confirm it completes under UAC.
src-r12: While running update tests, confirm the updates/applying-updates window stays visible and isn't lost behind other open windows.
src-2040: Use Help > Check for New Version and confirm Bloom reports your copy is up to date.
src-2050: Disconnect from the Internet, check for updates, and confirm Bloom shows the offline response without getting stuck.
src-tc105-steps-11-to-22-r15: After an offline update check, reconnect and confirm the stale 'can't check for new version offline' message clears.
src-2055: Install the previous Bloom version, update to current, and confirm the update doesn't download a full-installer-sized payload.
src-2060: Enable automatic updates and confirm the Bloom update runs automatically.
src-r18: Disable automatic updates and confirm the Bloom update waits for user approval before continuing.
src-r19: Update Bloom and confirm registration information is preserved (not lost) after the update.
src-2080: Choose Check for New Version and confirm Bloom reports a new version is available (after a few minutes).
src-2090: Check the Bloom shortcuts and confirm the Automatic Updates suggestion defaults to automatic download.
src-2100: Install the oldest Bloom version and run the updater to the current version, confirming the upgrade succeeds (BL-15577).
src-2110: With the Bloom program folder made unreadable, run Bloom and confirm a failure dialog links to Installation Troubleshooting.
src-tc7-r28: Set up a Windows VM (Edge, snapshot), install Bloom, and ensure the required .NET version is upgraded if inadequate.
src-tc357-r29: Start Bloom 6.3 on Windows 10 and verify Windows 10 is not supported starting in 6.3 (BL-15248).
src-tc8-r33: Delete a book's .htm file (keeping bookhtml.bak), restart Bloom, and verify it warns and restores from the recent backup.
src-2170: Delete both a book's .htm and bookhtml.bak files, restart Bloom, and verify it does not crash.
src-tc10-r35: Try opening a .bloomcollection from inside a ZIP file and verify Bloom tells you to unzip it first.
src-tc110-steps-1-to-7-r42: Submit a problem report and verify the email sends with the book zipped and a correct screenshot attached.
src-tc110-step-8-to-11-r43: Open the problem report and verify the reporter's registered email and name are pre-filled.
src-tc110-step-12-to-16-r44: Respond in YouTrack using @reporter and verify the response reaches the reporter's email.
src-tc110-step-17-to-20-r45: From the reporter's email, reply to the Bloom Team and verify it appears in YouTrack within about ten minutes.
src-tc93-r46: Report a problem, click Learn More, and verify there is no close button.
src-tc14-r47: On a fresh machine with old SIL/Bloom remnants removed, register an earlier version, upgrade, and verify registration info is saved.
src-tc359-r48: Intentionally corrupt a collection file, then boot Bloom and navigate to it, verifying error handling works.
src-tc12-r49: Disconnect from the internet, attempt to report a problem, and verify Bloom offers a friendly solution.

src-tc260-r620: Ensure the correct language is identified in the licensing error message.
src-tc261-r621: Ensure you can publish a licensed book in an authorized language (e.g. 'lwl' for SuperBible), unticking English first.
src-tc249-r624: Verify Spreadsheet import/export menu items are visible but disabled when Enterprise is off and the Spreadsheet experimental feature is on.
src-tc250-r625: In a Team Collection, verify Spreadsheet Export works on all books but Import only when the book is checked out.
src-7250b: Download a book from Bloom Library, export it to a spreadsheet, and import that into a new blank book.
src-tc483-r627: Confirm image descriptions in two languages are preserved on spreadsheet round-trip, both text and audio recordings.
src-tc484-r628: When importing the spreadsheet into a blank book, confirm images are brought in.
src-tc485-r629: Confirm text and audio in two languages are preserved on spreadsheet round-trip.
src-tc252-steps-1-to-33-r630: Record a whole-text-box page with segment markers, export to spreadsheet, and verify the segment markers appear in the spreadsheet.
src-tc252-steps-34-to-36-r631: Import the spreadsheet into a new book and verify the audio is still aligned to the segments you made.
src-tc252-steps-37-38-r632: Close the Talking Book tool and confirm the segment markers disappear.
src-tc253-steps-1-to-47-r633: Apply varied inline formatting (color, italics, etc.) to text, export to spreadsheet, and verify the formatting is preserved in Excel.
src-tc253-48-to-51-r634: Import the spreadsheet into a blank book and verify all the inline formatting round-tripped correctly.
src-tc56-import-origina-book-tc254-r635: Export a book, remove some pages from the spreadsheet, import back onto the original, and verify those pages are removed.
src-tc486-r636: Test spreadsheet 'Page Type' handling: page structure is preserved reimporting into the original book but not into a blank one.
src-tc487-r637: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of XMatter pages.
src-tc256-r638: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of comprehension quizzes.
src-tc257-r639: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of HTML5 widgets.
src-tc259-r640: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of activity pages.
src-tc258-r641: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of overlay pages.
src-tc255-r642: Test spreadsheet export/import handling of motion books.
src-7000: Confirm you can switch collections: the most recent 9 are listed, and you can choose another not on the list.
src-tc217-r646: Resize the Bloom window smaller and make sure it responds well — content shifts appropriately and scroll bars appear.
src-7010: Simulate first-install startup (clear AppData and collections), run the first-collection wizard, and confirm you can choose your UI language first.
src-7020: Test making a new collection later, after the first-install setup.
src-7030: Try all license options and confirm the CC icon and text update appropriately, with the icon disappearing for non-CC licenses.
src-7040: Confirm the License dialog's OK button is enabled only when the minimum required fields are filled.
src-7050: Smoke-test changing xmatter packs and confirm the change is reflected in your collection.
src-7070: Try all the help buttons and make sure each leads to a relevant topic (main tabs and the reader tools).
src-7080: Try creating a second collection with the same name and confirm you get a 'collection already exists' message.
src-7090: Try custom book and collection settings stylesheets.
src-7100: Switch the UI to another language and check several UI areas (some known untranslated areas are accepted).
src-7120: Check the behavior of each command in the Help menu.
src-7130: Quit Bloom from each tab and make sure no changes are lost when quitting from the Edit tab.
src-7140: Smoke-test the items in the Collection dropdown menu.
src-7210: Make an RTL book via special script settings; confirm it changes ePUB page order and justification (roman text stays LTR).
src-7230: Test the mechanism that copies the current license info to all images.
src-7290: Inspect a saved book's HTML and confirm there's no mention of 'ckeditor' or other user-interface leftovers.
src-tc353-r664: Test Bloom's correction of duplicate book IDs caused by File Explorer folder copies, including the Team Collection scenarios in BL-10512.
src-tc58-r667: Paste a Maithili title with a tricky surrogate pair, then change pages so the book files take that name.
src-8000: Navigate through a book with lots of big pictures and watch for performance problems.
src-tc230-r669: When navigating to new pages, ensure the correct thumbnail is highlighted.
src-tc231-r670: Publish Eric Ding's dictionary to ePUB, PDF, and BloomPUB, comparing performance with the previous Bloom version.
src-tc232-r671: Work through a huge Sign Language book (BL-10342), monitoring performance and watching for memory leakage during various operations.
src-8005: Ensure a collection containing many complex books (the 'very large collection') opens without undue delay.
src-tc234-r673: Move, add, delete, and duplicate pages in the Eric Ding dictionary and confirm speed is no worse than previous versions.
src-tc239-r674: Install the SBC English books BloomPack and move between books (editing or just clicking), confirming Bloom stays responsive.
src-8020: Randomly navigate the 127-page BL-7277 dictionary, make a PDF booklet, and ensure it succeeds without memory usage climbing.
src-tc241-r676: Run the BL-12756 performance tests (book selection, creation, duplicate page) and note performance compared to the previous version.
src-8030: Open the Very Large Collection of many books and check performance.
src-8040: Test a huge decodable reader sample-text collection (many or large files, or a mixture).
src-8050: Paste a large amount of text into a page and watch for performance problems (per BL-4579).
src-8060: Paste valid HTML into a page and confirm you just see the raw markup you pasted.
src-8070: Paste invalid XML into a page; you should just see the raw text (or Bloom restores a backup if unparsable).
src-8080: See how current Bloom handles the oldest book you can find, including publishing it to PDF, ePUB, and Bloom Reader.
src-8090: See how Bloom handles missing images: the cover shows a blank space and content pages show a 'picture missing' message.
src-8100: See how Bloom handles corrupt HTML/CSS: it shouldn't crash, other books stay usable, and a helpful message explains the problem.
src-8110: Unplug the internet during an update download; Bloom shouldn't crash or block future updates, ideally showing a helpful message.
src-8120: Test a collection on a network drive mapped to a drive letter (same physical computer).
src-8130: Test a collection on a shared network folder accessed by UNC path (no drive letter).
src-8140: Test a collection on a network drive hosted on a different physical computer.
src-8150: Test for an installation error when the machine's UI language is set to Dutch.
